title: Gemini: AI for School and Life

modules.title: Gemini Across Google: Where It Lives and What It Touches
modules.sub: Mapping Gemini's presence across every Google tool you use
modules.description: Learners take a guided tour of where Gemini shows up inside Docs, Gmail, Drive, and beyond, so nothing feels hidden or accidental. You identify which tools you already use daily and make a concrete plan for where Gemini fits into your existing habits. By the end, you have a personal map of the Google ecosystem with Gemini's entry points clearly marked.

modules.title: Supercharge Your Research and Note-Taking
modules.sub: Using Gemini to gather, summarize, and organize information faster
modules.description: You practice using Gemini inside Google Docs and Drive to summarize sources, pull out key ideas, and turn raw research into structured notes. The module walks you through real study scenarios — like prepping for an exam or starting a research paper — so the skills connect directly to your academic life. You leave with a repeatable research workflow you can apply to your next assignment.

modules.title: Drafting Emails, Essays, and Applications Without Losing Your Voice
modules.sub: Getting Gemini to write with you, not instead of you
modules.description: Learners use Gemini in Gmail and Docs to draft and refine written work, experimenting with prompts that preserve their personal tone rather than producing generic output. You compare Gemini-assisted drafts against your own writing to understand where AI helps and where it flattens your voice. The module ends with a practical framework for deciding when to lean on Gemini and when to write from scratch.

modules.title: Multimodal Magic: Working with Images, PDFs, and Audio
modules.sub: Going beyond text to analyze files, visuals, and recordings
modules.description: You upload real files — lecture slides, PDF readings, images, or audio clips — and use Gemini to extract meaning, generate summaries, and answer questions about the content. The module highlights how multimodal input changes what AI can do for you, especially when studying dense or visual material. You practice choosing the right input format for the task so you get the most useful output every time.

modules.title: Build Your Own Gemini Workflow for the Semester
modules.sub: Designing a personal AI system that survives past this course
modules.description: Learners map out a semester-long plan that slots Gemini into their real schedule — specific classes, deadlines, communication needs, and work habits. You make deliberate choices about which Gemini features to use routinely and which to skip, based on what you learned in earlier modules. The course closes with a documented, personalized workflow you can actually open on day one of next semester.

synopsis: If you already live in Google Docs, Gmail, and Drive, Gemini is already in your corner â you just haven't turned it on yet. This course shows you how to use it across your whole Google ecosystem to study smarter, communicate better, and actually get things done.
rationale: Google Workspace is ubiquitous in colleges and early workplaces, but students rarely know how deeply Gemini is now integrated across those tools or how to use it strategically rather than accidentally.
learning_outcome: After this course, you can use Gemini fluidly across Google's ecosystem to speed up research, writing, and communication in ways that directly improve your academic and professional output.
source_signals: Google supercharges machine learning tasks with TPU custom chip
nearest_existing: Gemini's Origins: From LaMDA to the Multimodal Era
